title: Kengyilia thoroldiana
intro: Kengyilia thoroldiana, also known as Thorold's grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as arthritis and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Kengyilia thoroldiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the form of dried herbs and extracts, to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Kengyilia thoroldiana is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Kengyilia, which is a group of perennial grasses. Kengyilia thoroldiana is further classified as a member of the tribe Triticeae, which includes various types of wheat and related grasses. The species is native to the Himalayan region, where it grows in mountainous areas and rocky terrain. Kengyilia thoroldiana is a relatively rare and poorly studied species, adding to the complexity of its classification.
